comment_text.sn3.24:1.5: Also asked by the brahmin Devahita at SN 7.13:7.2 and by Sakka at SN 11.16:2.4.
comment_text.sn3.24:1.6: Giving is not purely a matter of utilitarian calculation. It creates an emotional connection, which is why giving with one’s own hands is important. It’s not just about helping another; it’s also about elevating oneself.
comment_text.sn3.24:1.14: Note that, while the khattiyas were often said to be the warrior class, there is no difference here.

root_text.sn3.24:1.2: Ekamantaṁ nisinno kho rājā pasenadi kosalo bhagavantaṁ etadavoca:
root_text.sn3.24:1.6: “Aññaṁ kho etaṁ, mahārāja, kattha dānaṁ dātabbaṁ, aññaṁ panetaṁ kattha dinnaṁ mahapphalanti?
root_text.sn3.24:1.7: Sīlavato kho, mahārāja, dinnaṁ mahapphalaṁ, no tathā dussīle.
root_text.sn3.24:1.8: Tena hi, mahārāja, taññevettha paṭipucchissāmi. Yathā, te khameyya, tathā naṁ byākareyyāsi.
root_text.sn3.24:1.10: idha tyassa yuddhaṁ paccupaṭṭhitaṁ saṅgāmo samupabyūḷho.
root_text.sn3.24:1.11: Atha āgaccheyya khattiyakumāro asikkhito akatahattho akatayoggo akatūpāsano bhīru chambhī utrāsī palāyī.
root_text.sn3.24:1.12: Bhareyyāsi taṁ purisaṁ, attho ca te tādisena purisenā”ti?
root_text.sn3.24:1.13: “Nāhaṁ, bhante, bhareyyaṁ taṁ purisaṁ, na ca me attho tādisena purisenā”ti.
root_text.sn3.24:1.17: na ca me attho tādisena purisenā”ti.
root_text.sn3.24:2.2: idha tyassa yuddhaṁ paccupaṭṭhitaṁ saṅgāmo samupabyūḷho.
root_text.sn3.24:2.3: Atha āgaccheyya khattiyakumāro susikkhito katahattho katayoggo katūpāsano abhīru acchambhī anutrāsī apalāyī.
root_text.sn3.24:2.4: Bhareyyāsi taṁ purisaṁ, attho ca te tādisena purisenā”ti?
root_text.sn3.24:2.5: “Bhareyyāhaṁ, bhante, taṁ purisaṁ, attho ca me tādisena purisenā”ti.
root_text.sn3.24:2.8: atha āgaccheyya suddakumāro susikkhito katahattho katayoggo katūpāsano abhīru acchambhī anutrāsī apalāyī.
root_text.sn3.24:2.9: Bhareyyāsi taṁ purisaṁ, attho ca te tādisena purisenā”ti?
root_text.sn3.24:2.10: “Bhareyyāhaṁ, bhante, taṁ purisaṁ, attho ca me tādisena purisenā”ti.
root_text.sn3.24:3.1: “Evameva kho, mahārāja, yasmā kasmā cepi kulā agārasmā anagāriyaṁ pabbajito hoti, so ca hoti pañcaṅgavippahīno pañcaṅgasamannāgato, tasmiṁ dinnaṁ mahapphalaṁ hoti.
root_text.sn3.24:3.3: Kāmacchando pahīno hoti, byāpādo pahīno hoti, thinamiddhaṁ pahīnaṁ hoti, uddhaccakukkuccaṁ pahīnaṁ hoti, vicikicchā pahīnā hoti.
root_text.sn3.24:3.6: Asekkhena sīlakkhandhena samannāgato hoti, asekkhena samādhikkhandhena samannāgato hoti, asekkhena paññākkhandhena samannāgato hoti, asekkhena vimuttikkhandhena samannāgato hoti, asekkhena vimuttiñāṇadassanakkhandhena samannāgato hoti.

translation_text.sn3.24:1.2: Seated to one side, King Pasenadi said to the Buddha,
translation_text.sn3.24:1.3: “Sir, where should a gift be given?”
translation_text.sn3.24:1.4: “Wherever your heart feels inspired, great king.”
translation_text.sn3.24:1.5: “But sir, where is a gift very fruitful?”
translation_text.sn3.24:1.6: “Where a gift should be given is one thing, great king, but where a gift is very fruitful is another.
translation_text.sn3.24:1.7: A gift is very fruitful when it’s given to an ethical person, not so much to an unethical person.
translation_text.sn3.24:1.8: Well then, great king, I’ll ask you about this in return, and you can answer as you like.
translation_text.sn3.24:1.9: What do you think, great king?
translation_text.sn3.24:1.10: Suppose you were at war, ready to fight a battle.
translation_text.sn3.24:1.11: Then along comes an aristocrat youth who is untrained, inexpert, unfit, inexperienced. And he’s fearful, scared, nervous, quick to flee.
translation_text.sn3.24:1.12: Would you employ such a man? Would he be of any use to you?”
translation_text.sn3.24:1.13: “No, sir, I would have no use for such a man.”
translation_text.sn3.24:1.16: or a menial youth who was similar?”
translation_text.sn3.24:1.17: “No, sir, I would have no use for such a man.”
translation_text.sn3.24:2.2: Suppose you were at war, ready to fight a battle.
translation_text.sn3.24:2.3: Then along comes an aristocrat youth who is trained, expert, fit, experienced. And he’s fearless, brave, bold, standing his ground.
translation_text.sn3.24:2.4: Would you employ such a man? Would he be of any use to you?”
translation_text.sn3.24:2.5: “Yes, sir, I would have a use for such a man.”
translation_text.sn3.24:2.8: or a menial youth who was similar?
translation_text.sn3.24:2.9: Would you employ such a man? Would he be of any use to you?”
translation_text.sn3.24:2.10: “Yes, sir, I would have a use for such a man.”
translation_text.sn3.24:3.1: “In the same way, a gift to anyone who has given up five factors and possesses five factors is very fruitful, no matter what family they’ve gone forth from.
translation_text.sn3.24:3.2: What are the five factors they’ve given up?
translation_text.sn3.24:3.3: Sensual desire, ill will, dullness and drowsiness, restlessness and remorse, and doubt.
translation_text.sn3.24:3.4: These are the five factors they’ve given up.
translation_text.sn3.24:3.5: What are the five factors they possess?
translation_text.sn3.24:3.6: The entire spectrum of an adept’s ethics, immersion, wisdom, freedom, and knowledge and vision of freedom.
translation_text.sn3.24:3.7: These are the five factors they possess.
translation_text.sn3.24:3.8: I say that a gift to anyone who has given up these five factors and possesses these five factors is very fruitful.”
translation_text.sn3.24:3.9: That is what the Buddha said.
translation_text.sn3.24:3.10: Then the Holy One, the Teacher, went on to say:
translation_text.sn3.24:4.3: would be employed by a king going to war—
translation_text.sn3.24:4.4: one is not a coward by reason of birth.
translation_text.sn3.24:5.2: in the qualities of patience and gentleness,
translation_text.sn3.24:5.3: a clever person with noble conduct,
translation_text.sn3.24:5.4: should be venerated even if they’re low born.
translation_text.sn3.24:6.2: and settle learned people in them.
translation_text.sn3.24:6.3: You should set up water supplies in barren regions
translation_text.sn3.24:6.4: and passages in places hard to travel.
translation_text.sn3.24:7.3: should be given to the upright ones,
translation_text.sn3.24:7.4: with a clear and confident heart.
translation_text.sn3.24:8.2: its hundred peaks wreathed in lightning,
translation_text.sn3.24:8.3: pours down over the rich earth,
translation_text.sn3.24:9.3: should prepare a meal to satisfy
translation_text.sn3.24:10.4: like rain sent from the heavens.
translation_text.sn3.24:10.5: That stream of merit so abundant
